response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly accomplished web and full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems credentials, but is a poor fit for this C++ industrial automation role. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his skills section. The role's core requirements — deep C++/STL mastery, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443-4-1 / SDL), IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and industrial automation domain knowledge — are entirely absent from his profile.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience via micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic, using async I/O and low-latency techniques
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring through workshops and code kata sessions
REST API design and backend engineering experience across multiple stacks and production environments
concerns: Negligible C++ experience — approximately six months at National Instruments and one academic project; C++ is absent from his own skills list
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial cybersecurity standard; no mention of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, or DNP 3.0
Career is almost entirely web and SaaS; no industrial automation, on-premises control-system, or meaningful IoT domain background

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 knowledge
Strong distributed systems and engineering discipline
